concept.conceptName: Alkaline phosphatase|Catalytic Concentration|Moment in time|Blood, Serum or Plasma

en.name: Alkaline phosphatase (ALP) in blood
en.longDescription: ## Definition & Clinical Context Alkaline phosphatase (ALP) is a hydrolase enzyme that catalyses the removal of phosphate groups from various molecules. It is present throughout body tissues but is concentrated in the liver, bile ducts, kidney, bone, and placenta [1]. Elevated activity in blood is most often seen in cholestatic liver disease (bile duct obstruction) and in conditions with increased bone turnover (growth in children, Paget's disease, bone metastases); it is also physiologically increased in pregnancy [1]. The source data will typically be a single numeric result per patient-timestamp, produced by a biochemistry analyser on serum or plasma. It is collected in routine biochemistry as part of a liver panel. Results are reported as catalytic activity concentration; NPU recognises both **U/L** (the conventional unit, used by virtually all clinical labs) and **µkat/L** (the SI unit) [2]. The IFCC reference measurement procedure for alkaline phosphatase at 37 °C, listed by JCTLM, gives the conversion factor `1 U/L = 0.01667 µkat/L` [3]. **Note on isoenzymes.** ALP exists as several tissue-specific isoenzymes (liver, bone, intestinal, placental). This concept set covers the **total** ALP activity assayed in routine biochemistry — not the individual isoenzymes, which have their own LOINC concepts and are out of scope. ## Included Concepts The set contains three LOINC "Lab Test" concepts, all measuring total alkaline phosphatase catalytic activity in blood, differing only by specimen specification. - *3035995 / 6768-6 — Alkaline phosphatase [Enzymatic activity/volume] in Serum or Plasma (LOINC)*: the most widely used LOINC code for ALP, returned by routine biochemistry analysers. - *3001110 / 1783-0 — Alkaline phosphatase [Enzymatic activity/volume] in Blood (LOINC)*: used when the source reports ALP on whole blood without specifying serum/plasma. - *46235077 / 77141-0 — Alkaline phosphatase [Enzymatic activity/volume] in Serum, Plasma or Blood (LOINC)*: generic concept used when the matrix is not differentiated between serum/plasma and whole blood (e.g. aggregated multi-source feeds). **Note on units.** Recommended unit follows LOINC: **`[U]/L`** (concept 8645). The notations `U/L` and `IU/L` are used interchangeably in clinical practice and represent the same value mathematically — no conversion is needed between them. ## Excluded Concepts No concepts are explicitly excluded. The set is defined by anchoring on the LOINC group for total alkaline phosphatase catalytic activity in blood/serum/plasma and pulling in all its descendants. This naturally excludes: - **ALP isoenzymes**: liver-, bone-, intestinal-, placental-, renal-, regan-specific concepts (e.g. `1779-8 — Alkaline phosphatase.liver`, `1777-2 — Alkaline phosphatase.bone`). These are separate measurands. - **Isoenzyme ratios** like `Alkaline phosphatase.liver/Alkaline phosphatase.total` (`15015-1`) — out of scope. - **Heat stability tests** (`32351-9`, `32352-7`) — historical method-specific tests. - **Other specimens** such as cord blood (`55971-6`), body fluids, urine, dialysis fluid — out of scope for a "blood" concept set. - **Macromolecular ALP** (`100737-6`) — qualitative test for macro-ALP complexes. ## Mapping Notes - **Default concept** when the source reports "ALP", "alkaline phosphatase", "PAL" or similar without further specification (typical of routine biochemistry analysers): `3035995 / 6768-6 — Alkaline phosphatase [Enzymatic activity/volume] in Serum or Plasma`. By far the most common specimen for this assay. - **Whole blood without matrix tag**: if the source explicitly says "blood" (not serum/plasma), map to `3001110 / 1783-0 — Alkaline phosphatase [Enzymatic activity/volume] in Blood`. If the source aggregates results from both biochemistry and other workflows without a matrix tag, use `46235077 / 77141-0 — Alkaline phosphatase [Enzymatic activity/volume] in Serum, Plasma or Blood` as a generic fallback. - **Do not confuse with**: ALP isoenzymes (liver, bone, intestinal, etc.), isoenzyme ratios, or ALP in non-blood specimens — all belong to other concept sets. - **Method is not part of the mapping target**: when the source records the assay method (kinetic colorimetric, IFCC reference at 37 °C, etc.), preserve it in the measurement source value — it does not change the target concept. - **Unit naming**: source systems may report `U/L` or `IU/L`. Both correspond to the same physical quantity. Map them all to the recommended unit `[U]/L` (concept 8645). Values reported in `µkat/L` should be converted using `1 U/L = 0.01667 µkat/L` (i.e. multiply µkat/L by ~60 to get U/L) [3]. ## References [1] Regenstrief Institute. LOINC code 6768-6 — Alkaline phosphatase [Enzymatic activity/volume] in Serum or Plasma (Part description for "Alkaline phosphatase"). Available: https://loinc.org/6768-6/ [2] Nomenclature for Properties and Units (NPU). IFCC/IUPAC. NPU codes (current release). Available: https://npu-terminology.org/npu-database/ [3] Joint Committee for Traceability in Laboratory Medicine (JCTLM). Database of higher-order reference measurement procedures, reference materials and reference measurement services. BIPM/IFCC/ILAC. Available: https://www.jctlmdb.org/#/app/home
